title: Werkflow REST Transport Adapter
description: Subschema for `spec.transport.config` and `spec.operations[].transportSpecific` when transport.type=rest. Aligned with OpenAPI 3.1: a connector definition can be authored manually or generated from an existing OpenAPI document. Werkflow-specific extensions are namespaced as `x-werkflow-*` so a generated definition round-trips through OpenAPI tooling.

baseUrl.description: Base URL for the API. Resolved per-tenant via TenantEndpointResolver if a connector key is registered there; otherwise this default is used. May contain `${tenantCode}` for templating.

openApiSource.description: Optional. URL or path to the source OpenAPI 3.1 document. When present, the platform validates that operations defined here are consistent with the source spec and can re-import on changes.

defaultHeaders.description: Static headers applied to every request from this connector. Use `${var}` for process variable interpolation. Auth headers are NOT defined here — they come from the auth profile.

trustStoreRef.description: Secret key for a custom trust store. For mTLS or self-signed enterprise CAs.

ssrf.description: Per-connector SSRF policy. Cannot loosen the global SsrfGuard — only further restrict.

allowedHosts.description: If set, only these hosts are reachable from this connector. Hostname or CIDR.

redirectPolicy.description: Default `never` matches the engine's current behavior. `same-origin` is safer than `follow` if redirects are needed.

path.description: Path template appended to baseUrl. OpenAPI-style path parameters: `/orders/{orderId}/items`. Path parameters are sourced from the operation's input schema by name.

queryParameters.description: Query string parameters. Mapped from input schema fields. Supports `${var}` interpolation.

headers.description: Operation-specific headers. Merged on top of transportConfig.defaultHeaders. Supports `${var}` interpolation.

body.description: Request body specification. Only meaningful for POST/PUT/PATCH.

template.description: Body template. For JSON, a JSON string with `${var}` interpolation (numeric/boolean variables are emitted unquoted, strings are JSON-escaped). For form bodies, key=value template. For multipart, a structured spec — see multipartParts.

schemaRef.description: Optional reference to a schema in `spec.components.schemas`. Used to validate the resolved body before sending.

successStatusCodes.description: Status codes treated as success. Anything else falls through to errors[].

extractRoot.description: JSONPath into the response body to use as the operation's logical output. Useful when an API wraps results in `{ data: ..., metadata: ... }` — set extractRoot=`$.data` and the operation's output schema describes the unwrapped shape.

contentType.description: Expected response content type. Default: inferred from response Content-Type header.

x-werkflow-pagination.description: REST-specific pagination implementation. Only meaningful when the operation's top-level `pagination.strategy` is set.

x-werkflow-idempotency.description: Idempotency configuration for retries. The engine sends the configured header on every attempt of the same execution.

keyExpression.description: Expression producing the idempotency key. Default: `${execution.id}`. For workflow-instance-level idempotency, use `${execution.processInstanceId}:${operation.id}`.

x-werkflow-rateLimit.description: Operation-level rate limit override. Tighter than connector-level rateLimit if both are set.

valueExpression.description: Expression for the parameter's value. Defaults to a same-named field in the operation's input. Use `${var}` to reference process variables explicitly.

explode.description: OpenAPI explode behavior for arrays. true: ?tag=a&tag=b. false: ?tag=a,b.

RestPagination.description: Concrete pagination wiring for a REST operation. The top-level `pagination.strategy` selects which sub-shape applies.

cursorParameter.description: Query parameter or header name that carries the cursor on subsequent requests. e.g., 'pageToken', 'cursor', 'after'.

cursorPath.description: JSONPath into the response that yields the next cursor value. e.g., '$.nextPageToken', '$.meta.cursor.next'. When the path resolves to null/empty, pagination terminates.

itemsPath.description: JSONPath to the array of items in the response. e.g., '$.items', '$.data'.

pageSizeParameter.description: Optional query parameter name for page size. e.g., 'limit', 'pageSize'.

pageParameter.description: Query parameter for the page number. e.g., 'page'.

totalPagesPath.description: JSONPath to total pages count. Used to terminate the loop.

oneOf.title: Offset pagination (legacy — avoid for large datasets)

totalCountPath.description: JSONPath to total record count. Used to terminate the loop.
